steps.title: Locate the first median
steps.content:
Data set A has $41$ values, so its median is the $21$st value when the data are ordered.

$$\frac{41+1}{2}=21$$

From the table, positions $17$ through $25$ are all $3$, so the median of data set A is $3$.

steps.title: Locate the second median
steps.content: Removing the erroneous value $13$ leaves $40$ values. The median of data set B is the average of the $20$th and $21$st values. Those positions are still both $3$. $$\frac{3+3}{2}=3$$ The answer is C.
